question: Which country won the first-ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
funFact: Uruguay hosted the inaugural tournament in 1930 and defeated Argentina 4-2 in the final.

question: Which nation has won the most FIFA World Cup titles in history?
funFact: Brazil has won the World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002) and is the only team to play in every tournament.

question: How many host nations are organizing the FIFA World Cup 2026?
funFact: The 2026 World Cup will be jointly hosted by three nations: Canada, Mexico, and the United States.

question: How many teams will compete in the expanded FIFA World Cup 2026?
funFact: The 2026 edition will expand the tournament format from 32 teams to 48 teams for the first time.

question: Who is the all-time top goal scorer in FIFA World Cup history?
funFact: German forward Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cup tournaments (2002-2014).

question: Who holds the record for the most goals scored in a single FIFA World Cup tournament?
funFact: French striker Just Fontaine scored an incredible 13 goals in just six games during the 1958 World Cup.

question: Which historic stadium is set to host the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match?
funFact: MetLife Stadium (New York/New Jersey) was chosen to host the final on July 19, 2026.

question: Which stadium has hosted the FIFA World Cup Final twice, in 1970 and 1986?
funFact: Estadio Azteca in Mexico City hosted Pelé's Brazil victory in 1970 and Maradona's Argentina triumph in 1986.

question: Who is the oldest player to ever play in a FIFA World Cup match?
funFact: Egyptian goalkeeper Essam El-Hadary played in the 2018 World Cup at the age of 45 years and 161 days.

question: Which country has played in the most World Cup Finals without ever winning the trophy?
funFact: The Netherlands reached the World Cup Final three times (1974, 1978, 2010) but lost all three.

question: Which team became the first African nation to reach the World Cup semi-finals in 2022?
funFact: Morocco defeated Spain and Portugal in the knockout stages to reach the historic semi-final in Qatar.

question: Which country hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
funFact: Qatar was the first Middle Eastern country to host a FIFA World Cup.

question: Which player won the Golden Ball award for best player at both the 2014 and 2022 World Cups?
funFact: Lionel Messi is the only player to win the Golden Ball twice since the award was introduced in 1982.

question: Who was the president of FIFA who helped found the World Cup and has the trophy named after him?
funFact: Jules Rimet was the FIFA President who served for 33 years and initialized the inaugural 1930 tournament.

question: Which Qatari stadium featured a design constructed out of 974 shipping containers?
funFact: Stadium 974 in Doha was built using recycled shipping containers and was fully dismantled after the 2022 tournament.
